commands.review_summary:
The PR adds realpath-aware guards to stale OAuth profile shadow doctor repair and a regression test for child agent auth stores symlinked to the main auth store.

PR surface: Source +33, Tests +36. Total +69 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main compares path strings during scan and saves through a symlink-following JSON writer during repair. A focused repro is a main auth store plus child `auth-profiles.json` symlink to it, then running the stale-shadow scan/repair path on current main.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated code repair is needed; the remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer proof from a real branch/merge-ref run plus normal CI review.

target.title: fix(doctor): skip shared auth symlink shadows
target.body:
Fixes #89048

### Summary

- Resolve auth-store real paths before stale OAuth shadow repair treats an agent store as local.
- Skip child `auth-profiles.json` stores that resolve to the main agent auth store.
- Re-check the symlink/realpath condition before saving during repair.
- Add a regression test for child agent auth stores symlinked to main.

### Root cause

The stale OAuth profile shadow repair compared `auth-profiles.json` locations by normalized path strings. That misses the deployment shape where:

```text
~/.openclaw/agents/<agent>/agent/auth-profiles.json
  -> ~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json
```

The child path and main path are different strings, but the same file after symlink resolution. Without a realpath guard, doctor can treat the shared main store as a local child shadow and save a pruned profile set through the symlink.

### What changed

- Added realpath-aware auth-store identity checks to stale OAuth profile shadow scan/repair.
- Preserved existing behavior for real local child auth stores.
- Left legacy sidecar OAuth migration behavior unchanged.
- Added a regression test proving symlinked child auth stores are skipped and the main OAuth profile remains intact.

### Test plan

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/stale-oauth-profile-shadows.test.ts
# passed: 1 file, 9 tests

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
# passed
```

`node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs` was also attempted locally, but I stopped it after it ran silently for more than seven minutes. The targeted doctor-auth shard above covers the changed code path.

### Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Prevent `openclaw doctor --fix` stale OAuth shadow repair from treating a non-main agent `auth-profiles.json` symlink as a separate local child store and pruning the shared `openai-codex` OAuth profile through that symlink.
- Real environment tested: Linux VPS running OpenClaw `2026.5.28` with a managed gateway, default model `openai/gpt-5.5`, `openai-codex` OAuth, and multiple Telegram/direct agents whose auth stores inherit from `~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Verified the repaired production deployment after applying the local workaround that isolated child auth-store symlinks before `doctor --fix`, restored Codex OAuth, and re-linked agents to the main auth store. Commands run from the server were:

```bash
node /home/ubuntu/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/openclaw.mjs --version
node /home/ubuntu/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/openclaw.mjs models status --json
node /home/ubuntu/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/openclaw.mjs models auth list --provider openai-codex --json
node - <<'NODE'
const fs = require("fs");
const path = require("path");
const root = "/home/ubuntu/.openclaw/agents";
const main = path.join(root, "main/agent/auth-profiles.json");
for (const agent of fs.readdirSync(root).sort()) {
  if (agent === "main") continue;
  const file = path.join(root, agent, "agent/auth-profiles.json");
  try {
    const stat = fs.lstatSync(file);
    if (!stat.isSymbolicLink()) continue;
    const target = path.resolve(path.dirname(file), fs.readlinkSync(file));
    if (target === main) console.log(`${agent}: symlink-to-main-auth-store`);
  } catch {}
}
NODE
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
openclaw_version=OpenClaw 2026.5.28 (e932160)

main_auth_store=present
registered_agent_auth_links
builder: symlink-to-main-auth-store
guard: symlink-to-main-auth-store
harvey: symlink-to-main-auth-store
jobs: symlink-to-main-auth-store
kt: symlink-to-main-auth-store
rand: symlink-to-main-auth-store
ryan: symlink-to-main-auth-store
streamsathi: symlink-to-main-auth-store
trader: symlink-to-main-auth-store

models status --json redacted summary:
{
  "defaultModel": "openai/gpt-5.5",
  "imageModel": "openai/gpt-image-2",
  "authStorePath": "/home/ubuntu/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json",
  "providersWithOAuth": ["anthropic (1)", "google (1)", "openai-codex (1)"],
  "runtimeAuthRoutes": [
    {
      "provider": "openai",
      "runtime": "codex",
      "authProvider": "openai-codex",
      "status": "usable",
      "effective": {
        "kind": "profiles",
        "detail": "~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json"
      }
    }
  ],
  "unusableProfiles": []
}

models auth list --provider openai-codex --json redacted summary:
{
  "agentId": "main",
  "agentDir": "~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent",
  "provider": "openai-codex",
  "profiles": [
    {
      "id": "openai-codex:<email>",
      "provider": "openai-codex",
      "type": "oauth",
      "label": "openai-codex:<email> (<email>)",
      "email": "<email>",
      "expiresAt": "2026-06-11T08:04:31.880Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The main auth store is present, non-main agents are again linked to the shared main auth store, `models status` reports the OpenAI Codex runtime auth route as `usable`, and `models auth list --provider openai-codex` shows a live OAuth profile with no unusable profiles. This is the state that was broken after the update/doctor run before the workaround.
- What was not tested: I did not install this PR branch directly onto the production VPS. The branch-specific guard is covered by the regression test; the live proof covers the real repaired deployment and auth-store shape that caused the incident.

### Risk

Low. The change should only prevent doctor from treating a shared/symlinked main auth store as a removable local child shadow. Normal stale local child OAuth shadows should still be detected and repaired.
target.author: jbetala7
